2487	// TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected verifies that when execute-bead
2488	// invokes the embedded-agent harness, its session/telemetry runtime state is
2489	// redirected into a DDx-owned directory inside the execution bundle instead
2490	// of being written at the worktree root. Regression guard for ddx-cba2dc64.
2491	func TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected(t *testing.T) {
2492		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2493			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
2494			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
2495		}
2496	
2497		// Snapshot of wtPath root contents captured during Run so the assertion
2498		// can run before ExecuteBead removes the worktree on return.
2499		var wtPathDuringRun string
2500		var wtRootBefore []string
2501		var wtRootAfter []string
2502		var sessionLogDirSeen string
2503		simulatedSessionFile := "agent-embedded-session.jsonl"
2504	
2505		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{
2506			result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "agent"},
2507			sideEffect: func(opts agent.RunOptions) error {
2508				wtPathDuringRun = opts.WorkDir
2509				sessionLogDirSeen = opts.SessionLogDir
2510	
2511				// Capture worktree root listing before simulating writes.
2512				entries, err := os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
2513				if err != nil {
2514					return err
2515				}
2516				for _, e := range entries {
2517					wtRootBefore = append(wtRootBefore, e.Name())
2518				}
2519	
2520				// Simulate the embedded-agent harness writing runtime state.
2521				// It MUST land in opts.SessionLogDir, not opts.WorkDir. If the
2522				// execute-bead wiring is broken and SessionLogDir is empty or the
2523				// worktree root, this write will land at the worktree root and
2524				// the post-check below will catch it.
2525				if opts.SessionLogDir == "" {
2526					return fmt.Errorf("embedded agent runner received empty SessionLogDir; runtime state would land at worktree root")
2527				}
2528				if err := os.MkdirAll(opts.SessionLogDir, 0o755); err != nil {
2529					return err
2530				}
2531				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(opts.SessionLogDir, simulatedSessionFile), []byte(`{"event":"started"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
2532					return err
2533				}
2534	
2535				// Capture worktree root listing after simulated writes.
2536				entries, err = os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
2537				if err != nil {
2538					return err
2539				}
2540				for _, e := range entries {
2541					wtRootAfter = append(wtRootAfter, e.Name())
2542				}
2543				return nil
2544			},
2545		}
2546		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2547	
2548		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--harness", "agent")
2549		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome, "execute-bead should succeed for this test")
2550	
2551		// The runner must have received a SessionLogDir override.
2552		require.NotEmpty(t, sessionLogDirSeen, "execute-bead must pass a SessionLogDir to the embedded harness")
2553	
2554		// The override must point inside the tracked execution bundle, not at
2555		// the worktree root.
2556		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir, "execute-bead must record an execution bundle dir")
2557		bundleAbs := filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir))
2558		absLog, err := filepath.Abs(sessionLogDirSeen)
2559		require.NoError(t, err)
2560		absBundle, err := filepath.Abs(bundleAbs)
2561		require.NoError(t, err)
2562		assert.Truef(t, strings.HasPrefix(absLog, absBundle+string(filepath.Separator)),
2563			"SessionLogDir (%s) must be inside the execution bundle (%s)", absLog, absBundle)
2564		assert.Equal(t, "embedded", filepath.Base(absLog),
2565			"SessionLogDir must be the bundle's embedded/ subdirectory")
2566	
2567		// The worktree root must not gain any files during the run.
2568		require.NotEmpty(t, wtPathDuringRun)
2569		assert.Equal(t, wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter,
2570			"worktree root entries must not change while the embedded harness runs (before=%v after=%v)",
2571			wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter)
2572		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, simulatedSessionFile,
2573			"simulated embedded session file must not land at the worktree root")
2574		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, ".agent-session.json",
2575			"embedded agent must not write .agent-session.json at the worktree root")
2576	
2577		// The simulated session file must exist at the redirected location.
2578		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(sessionLogDirSeen, simulatedSessionFile))
2579	}

22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}

36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}

64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}

127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)

132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}

147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}

224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,

843		// dirty, HEAD is unchanged.

217	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
218	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
219	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
220	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
221		if exitCode != 0 {
222			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
223		}
224	
225		switch outcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
230		case "error":
231			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
232		case "preserved":
233			switch reason {
234			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
235				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
236			case "post-run checks failed":
237				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
238			case RatchetPreserveReason:
239				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
240			default:
241				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
242				// explicitly requested no merge.
243				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
244			}
245		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
246			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
247		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
248			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
249		default:
250			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
251		}
252	}

175	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
176		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
177	
178		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
179		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
180		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
181		if res.ExitCode != 0 && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
182			landing.Outcome = "error"
183			switch {
184			case res.Reason != "":
185				landing.Reason = res.Reason
186			case res.Error != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Error
188			default:
189				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
190			}
191			return landing, nil
192		}
193	
194		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
195		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
196			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
197			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
198				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
199			} else {
200				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
201			}
202			return landing, nil
203		}
204	
205		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
206		if res.ExitCode != 0 {
207			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
208			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
209				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
210			}
211			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
212			landing.PreserveRef = ref
213			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
214			return landing, nil
215		}
216	
217		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
218		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
219		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
220		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
221		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
222		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
223		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
224		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
225			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
226			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
227		)
228		if gateErr != nil {
229			return nil, gateErr
230		}
231		gateResults := res.GateResults
232		landing.GateResults = gateResults
233		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
234		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
235		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
236		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
237	
238		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
239		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
240		// them from generic command failures.
241		if anyGateFailed {
242			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
243			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
244				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
245			}
246			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
247			landing.PreserveRef = ref
248			if anyRatchetFailed {
249				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
250			} else {
251				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
252			}
253			return landing, nil
254		}
255	
256		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
257		if opts.NoMerge {
258			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
259			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
260				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
261			}
262			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
263			landing.PreserveRef = ref
